task_completion.description: Set up the Asteroid spacecraft and Sun-Jupiter point-mass force model
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI or script showing a Spacecraft (e.g., named 'Asteroid') and a Force Model configured with Point Masses for the Sun and Jupiter.

task_completion.description: Create a Sun-Jupiter rotating coordinate system
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT Coordinate System properties showing Origin set to Sun, Axes Type set to ObjectReferenced, Primary Object set to Sun, Secondary Object set to Jupiter, and X-axis pointing from Sun to Jupiter.

task_completion.description: Configure a ReportFile to output simulation time and absolute distance to Jupiter
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT ReportFile properties or script showing the selected parameters for output, specifically including simulation time and a distance metric to Jupiter (e.g., Asteroid.Jupiter.RMAG).

task_completion.description: Configure the Propagate command for 200 years
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT Propagate command properties or script showing a stopping condition of exactly 200 years or 73000 days.

task_completion.description: Save the script to ~/GMAT_output/tadpole_mission.script and run it
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal, file manager, or GMAT GUI showing the script saved as 'tadpole_mission.script' in the '~/GMAT_output/' directory, and evidence of execution (e.g., GMAT message window showing a completed run or the resulting report file being generated).

task_completion.description: Write the min and max distance results to tadpole_metrics.json
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal (e.g., using 'cat') or text editor showing the contents of '~/GMAT_output/tadpole_metrics.json' containing exactly the keys 'min_jupiter_dist_km' and 'max_jupiter_dist_km' with numerical values.

integrity.description: Agent actually used GMAT to create the mission or wrote a genuine GMAT script
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the GMAT GUI or a text editor containing valid GMAT script syntax, rather than just faking the final output files.

integrity.description: Agent ran the simulation and parsed real output
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of the generated GMAT report file or a script/command used to parse the report file, proving the min/max values were extracted from actual simulation data.

integrity.description: The final JSON contains calculated numerical values, not placeholders
integrity.visual_evidence: The values for 'min_jupiter_dist_km' and 'max_jupiter_dist_km' in the JSON file are actual numbers, not copied text like '<value>' from the prompt.
